JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTED BY: KATHRYN LLOYD, #5955, 925.548.9114.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING IN ITS ENTIRETY ON DEFENDANT OLKS, INC. DBA OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION AND TESTING OF PROPERTY; REQUEST FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INSPECTION OF PROPERTY IS DENIED. THE RELIEF SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT IS NOT LIMITED TO INSPECTING OR TESTING, BUT RATHER SEEKS PERMISSION TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO PLAINTIFFS' HOME. DEFENDANT HAS CITED NO AUTHORITY UNDER CCP SECTION 2031.010(C) THAT WOULD COMPEL A PARTY TO ALLOW THE INSPECTING PARTY TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS AND RECREATE HAZARDOUS CONDITIONS SUCH AS THOSE REQUESTED HERE. FURTHER, THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED THE EXTENT OF THE ALTERATIONS DEFENDANT PROPOSES TO MAKE TO PLAINTIFFS' HOME IN ORDER TO ATTEMPT TO RECREATE PREEXISTING CONDITIONS AND THE UNCERTAINTY THAT THE ALTERATIONS WILL RESULT IN A TEST THAT IS ACCURATE, AND BALANCED THAT AGAINST THE POSSIBLE PREJUDICIAL IMPACT TO PLAINTIFFS OF RECREATING HAZARDOUS CONDITIONS IN THEIR HOME. UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE REQUEST FOR FURTHER INSPECTION IS UNWARRANTED AND IS DENIED. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: ALICE CHANG, CSR # 13654, TEL. 415.591.3333.

Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard to plaintiff's safety. The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Craig decided to cap the second flue without ever consulting with plaintiff or investigating whether the flue was still in operation. (See Defendants' UMF 31-34; Plaintiffs' UMF 18 (Kolderup Declaration 16.) The Court rejects defendant's argument that plaintiff has no evidence that defendant's director, officer, or managing agent engaged in oppressive conduct or ratified such conduct. This issue was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion Andmoton To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Robert Maiolo, For The Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant OLKS, INC., doing business as OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S Motion to Compel the further Continued Deposition of cross defendant Robert Maiolo, for the Production of Documents, and for Sanctions is DENIED.
Cross defendant Maiolo, a self-employed plumber, is a party here under an equitable indemnity cross-complaint. Two depositions of him have already taken place, one noticed by defendant OKLS on July 30, 2012, and the other noticed by plaintiffs on April 23, 2013. Defendant OKLS's stated reasons in its motion papers here for an even further or, to use its terminology, continued, deposition are somewhat opaque, particularily given the extensive discovery opportunities already afforded it by the two earlier depositions of cross defendant Maiolo. Defendant had a long period of time between the two depositions to prepare for the second and to cover in the second all of issues and areas that it needed to, but which it now may be trying to address. In addition, the further deposition requested would, if granted, run afoul of the spirit, if not the precise letter, of the seven hour deposition limit rule of C.C.P section 2025.290. Under the circumstances, there does not appear to be the requisite good cause now to warrant a deviation from that provision's obligations. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian A. Colley; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant Olks, Inc. Doing Business As Okell'S Fireplace'S Motion To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Request For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar May-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patrick O'Brien, Esq. of Daniel Crowley & Associates, Tel. 707.525.8999, for defendant and moving party;
Gary A. Angel, Esq. of the Law Office of Gary A. Angel, Tel. 415.788.5935, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling in its entirety on DEFENDANT OLKS, INC. DBA OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION AND TESTING OF PROPERTY; REQUEST FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS. The motion to compel further inspection of property is denied. The relief sought by Defendant is not limited to inspecting or testing, but rather seeks permission to make modifications to Plaintiffs' home. Defendant has cited no authority under CCP section 2031.010(c) that would compel a party to allow the inspecting party to make modifications and recreate hazardous conditions such as those requested here. Further, the Court has considered the extent of the alterations Defendant proposes to make to Plaintiffs' home in order to attempt to recreate preexisting conditions and the uncertainty that the alterations will result in a test that is accurate, and balanced that against the possible prejudicial impact to Plaintiffs of recreating hazardous conditions in their home. Under the circumstances, the request for further inspection is unwarranted and is denied. No monetary sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR # 13654, Tel. 415.591.3333.
